Cyrtochilum macasense is native to Ecuador.

intro: Cyrtochilum macasense, commonly known as Maca Orchid, is a species of orchid native to the Andean region of South America. This plant has been traditionally used for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may help to support reproductive and hormonal balance, as well as alleviate symptoms of menopause and fatigue. The bioactive constituents of Cyrtochilum macasense include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are believed to be responsible for its medicinal properties. Cyrtochilum macasense is often used to make herbal supplements, such as capsules and teas, as well as tinctures and extracts, which are marketed for their potential to promote overall health and well-being.
